name: cloud-guard
description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for DIRECT rented-GPU provider CLI / API usage — ALL runpod / vast.ai access routes through `hexa cloud` (commons @D g8), which now wraps the FULL pod lifecycle (rent · down · list/status · exec · run · nohup · poll · tail · copy-to/from · preflight). Five orthogonal detectors run on every Bash command: (a) pod-CLI + verb pair scan anywhere in the command — a provider CLI (`runpodctl` · `vastai` · `vast`) followed by ANY recognized verb is denied: read (list/get/show/search/status), lifecycle (create/start/stop/remove/destroy/launch/config/project/version), AND remote-exec/transfer (exec/ssh/send/receive/scp/copy/port/attach-ssh) — 0.3.0 closes the prior read+lifecycle pass-through (e.g. `runpodctl pod list` that leaked to the pool); the following-verb requirement keeps `which runpodctl` / `man vastai` / `echo vast` (no verb) passing; (b) ssh/scp/rsync/sftp against `*.runpod.io` / `proxy.runpod.net` / `*.vast.ai` hosts; (c) curl/wget/http(ie)/xh against `api.runpod.io` / `api.runpod.ai` / `rest.runpod.io` / `runpod.io/graphql` / `console.vast.ai` / `api.vast.ai` / `vast.ai/api` URLs; (d) dispatcher-script content scan — when the command invokes `bash <path>` / `sh <path>` / `zsh <path>` / `dash <path>` with a positional script path, the script file is read and detectors (a)/(b)/(c) re-run on its body, closing the workaround of writing a dispatch.sh containing raw `runpodctl ssh` then calling `bash dispatch.sh`; (e) inline-runtime provider-SDK probe — `python(3)?`/`node`/`deno`/`bun` with `-c`/`-e` whose body imports the runpod / vastai SDK (raw API use); committed SDK code (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through (mirrors ai-api-guard). Shell separators (; & | () ` " ' \) normalized to whitespace before tokenizing — `cd /x && runpodctl ssh …`, `bash -c '… vast exec …'`, env-prefixes all caught. Redirects to `hexa cloud {rent|down|list|run|nohup|poll|copy-to|copy-from|preflight}` with per-verb mapping. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_cloud_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.
